summary: Renamed a group of name-entry, persistent-save, rumble, and stats/diagram functions and data symbols from address-based placeholders to semantic names, updated matching declarations/comments in headers and C stubs, and introduced a provisional NameData struct for player/name stat fields. The PR was mostly metadata/naming cleanup with no reported GALE01 decomp output changes.
what_changed: Renamed gmMainLib_8015CC64 to GetPersistentFighterData, PersistentNameData to GetPersistentNameData, and gmMainLib_8015ED3C to GetRumbleSettingOfPort in symbols, C definitions, headers, and an internal call site.
Renamed mnName address placeholders to semantic name-list helpers, including DeleteName, mnName_GetPageCount, mnName_GetColumnCount, and IsNameNotAllowed.

Renamed several mnNameNew symbols/data objects related to name entry: PickAutoName, NameContainsOnlySpaces, WriteCharactersForNameAtIndex, AddCharacterToName, InitNameEntryUIState, mnNameNew_CurrentNameText, mnNameNew_SpaceCharacter, and mnNameNew_NullCharacter.
Renamed name-list global data from address-style mnName_804D6C00 and mnName_804D6C04 to NotAllowedNamesList and AutoNamesList.

Renamed mnDiagram stat-calculation placeholders to GetHitPercentage, GetPlayPercentage, GetAveragePlayerCount, GetNameTotalKOs, GetNameTotalFalls, GetFighterTotalKOs, and GetFighterTotalFalls in symbols, headers, and C placeholder comments.

Added struct NameData in src/melee/gm/types.h with documented stat-like fields such as self_destructs, attacks_hit, attacks_total, damage_given, damage_taken, play_time, distances, coin fields, rumble_toggle, and a text representation pointer.
In mnHyaku, replaced the literal 0x80 argument to mn_8022F3D8 with MOBJ_MASK and added a TODO noting that 0xFF appears to signal a stopped animation.
smart_moves: Kept config/GALE01/symbols.txt, headers, C definitions, and placeholder comments synchronized so renamed symbols remain searchable and declarations stay consistent.
Used behavior-oriented names for simple accessors and predicates, making call sites more readable without requiring full decompilation of every function.
Preserved unknown signatures with UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS for not-yet-decompiled mnName and mnDiagram routines, avoiding unsupported type claims while still improving symbol names.
Introduced NameData as a central type for recurring persistent name/stat fields, with comments documenting known offsets and uncertainty about overlap with character stats.
Replaced a raw mask literal with MOBJ_MASK in mnHyaku, which improves intent without changing behavior.
decomp_lessons: Semantic renames can be valuable even when functions remain undecompiled; updating symbol maps and headers provides stable anchors for future work.
For persistent save/name data, getters should be named by returned domain object rather than by address, e.g. GetPersistentFighterData and GetPersistentNameData.
When a struct is only partially understood, field names plus offset comments and uncertainty notes are better than leaving repeated raw offsets unexplained.
Stat-related menu routines can often be named from their displayed output role, such as hit percentage, play percentage, KO totals, and fall totals.
Predicate and action names such as IsNameUnique, IsNameValid, IsNameNotAllowed, DeleteName, PickAutoName, and AddCharacterToName create a readable vocabulary for name-entry logic.
Small constant cleanups should use existing symbolic constants when known, but TODO comments are appropriate when a nearby argument such as 0xFF still has unclear semantics.
naming_conventions: Global identified functions in this slice use PascalCase for domain-level helpers: GetPersistentFighterData, GetPersistentNameData, GetRumbleSettingOfPort, DeleteName, IsNameNotAllowed.
Module-scoped menu helpers may retain a module prefix with PascalCase suffixes, as in mnName_GetPageCount and mnName_GetColumnCount.
Boolean/predicate helpers use Is*/Contains* style names: IsNameUnique, IsNameValid, IsNameNotAllowed, NameContainsOnlySpaces.
Getter-style stat functions use Get + measured quantity: GetHitPercentage, GetPlayPercentage, GetAveragePlayerCount, GetNameTotalKOs, GetFighterTotalFalls.
Name-entry globals use descriptive object names rather than raw BSS/SBSS addresses: NotAllowedNamesList, AutoNamesList, mnNameNew_CurrentNameText, mnNameNew_SpaceCharacter, mnNameNew_NullCharacter.
Action helpers use verbs that describe side effects: DeleteName, CreateNameAtIndex, WriteCharactersForNameAtIndex, AddCharacterToName, InitNameEntryUIState.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-matching rewrite was evident; the decomp.dev bot reported 'No changes' for GALE01.
Renames were applied without changing known function sizes or addresses in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
Header prototypes for still-unknown functions remained UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS, limiting churn while enabling symbol-name progress.
A raw 0x80 argument was replaced with MOBJ_MASK in mnHyaku, suggesting use of named constants to clarify matching code when the emitted code remains unchanged.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp.dev comment reported: 'Report for GALE01 ... No changes', indicating no observed decomp output/regression changes for the tested version.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that use or refine struct NameData field offsets and padding.
Search for implementations of GetHitPercentage and GetPlayPercentage to confirm signatures and return types.
Search for later renames of mnNameNew_8023B0F8 through mnNameNew_8023E32C surrounding the renamed name-entry helpers.
Check whether GetPersistentFighterData and GetPersistentNameData later receive typed return values instead of void*.
Investigate mn_8022F3D8 and the 0xFF stopped-animation TODO in mnHyaku for a better named constant.
